qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF and path traversal via unsanitized OpenAPI tool path parameters
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same FastMCP OpenAPIProvider RequestDirector._build_url root cause: attacker-controlled path parameters are substituted without URL encoding and then passed to urljoin, allowing traversal to unintended backend endpoints with provider credentials.

selected_finding.title: SSRF and path traversal via unsanitized OpenAPI tool path parameters

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code and mechanism, and the copied PoC reached an unintended backend endpoint through an OpenAPI path parameter traversal, returning secret data through the FastMCP OpenAPI tool response.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-vv7q-7jx5-f767 describes FastMCP OpenAPIProvider RequestDirector._build_url substituting path parameters without URL encoding before urljoin resolves ../ traversal.
openapi-ssrf-path-traversal/vulnerability_report.json cites src/fastmcp/utilities/openapi/director.py:194-216 and describes the same raw substitution plus urljoin source-to-sink path.
.judge/poc_runs/openapi-ssrf-path-traversal/run.log records installation of FastMCP v3.1.1 and confirms the vulnerable _build_url code uses raw substitution without URL encoding.

.judge/poc_runs/openapi-ssrf-path-traversal/run.log shows the OpenAPI getItem tool called with item_id ../../../secret returned EVOHUNT_SECRET_DATA_CONFIRMED from /secret, and the backend request log records /secret being reached.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC pinned FastMCP to vulnerable v3.1.1, confirmed _build_url used raw substitution, called the OpenAPI getItem tool with item_id ../../../secret, and received the backend /secret response containing EVOHUNT_SECRET_DATA_CONFIRMED.
